集训3 · 假期复习任务卡 · Day 2 of 24

Sandcastle by the Lake

📖 Reading

310 words · 28 target words · listen while you read

Tier 1（8词） Tier 2（9词） Tier 3（11词）

Leo's family had just decided to move to a small house at the edge of town. At first, Leo thought life there would be boring. There was nothing to do but sit near the old stone wall around the garden and watch the birds.

Then he met Ana, the girl next door. She had a fluffy puppy that followed her everywhere, and she was a cheerful character who always had a new plan. One afternoon, Ana sent Leo a message: "Come to the lake after school. I have an idea!"

The school day felt slow, but Leo did not hurry — as soon as the bell rang, he ran all the way there. He pushed open the old gate by the water and found Ana waiting, setting up a small tent for shade.

"Let's build something amazing, and race to see who finishes first!" Ana said. Leo agreed, and with quick hands, he began piling wet sand into a tower. He worked so fast that he was careless, and one side of his tower fell apart. A tear filled his eye.

Ana just smiled. "Let's fix this part together," she said. So they patted the sand until it was soft and smooth, and piece by piece, a tower rose between them.

By evening, they had built a giant castle with tall, proud towers. Ana explained her trick: wet sand at the bottom, dry sand on top. Leo listened closely, and soon he could hear the little waves lapping nearby. Under the rising moon, it looked like something from an old storybook.

When Ana's father came to look, he smiled wide. "You two are so smart!" he said, and he congratulated them both. Leo felt full of joy. He had been upset about the change at first, but now he knew this new town was going to be wonderful after all.

🔍 Reading Comprehension

5 questions · tap to check your answer

1In "He worked so fast that he was careless, and one side of his tower fell apart," what does careless mean?
B — Leo rushed and was not careful, which is why his tower fell apart.
2In "Ana explained her trick: wet sand at the bottom, dry sand on top," what does explained mean?
A — Ana told Leo about her method so he could understand how it worked.
3In "He pushed open the old gate by the water and found Ana waiting," what does gate mean?
C — a gate is an opening in a fence or wall that Leo had to push open to get through.
4Why did the first side of Leo's tower fall apart?
C — the story says he worked so fast that he was careless, and that is why the side fell apart.
5How did Leo feel about his new town by the end of the story?
D — after building the castle with Ana, Leo felt full of joy and knew the new town was going to be wonderful.
🔁
